page: Amazon Web Services g6e.xlarge is a graphics-intensive virtual machine instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz, configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, it includes a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPU with 45 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 250 GB NVMe SSD, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in large language model (LLM) inference workloads. The instance achieves top 10% scores in prompt processing and text generation for both small (135M) and medium (7B) models, processing 6130.77 tokens per second for 7B prompt processing and 134.56 tokens per second for 7B text generation. Single-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% via stress-ng. However, multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into average or weak categories, with multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Redis SET operations scoring in the bottom 25%.

Given the integration of a high-capacity 45 GB VRAM GPU alongside a modest 4 vCPU allocation, this instance offers a specialized hardware profile. It provides high resource density for GPU-bound tasks without requiring a high vCPU count, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that do not scale heavily on CPU cores but demand substantial graphics memory. The instance is highly suited for small to medium LLM inference, graphics-intensive rendering, and GPU-accelerated machine learning tasks, whereas it is less optimal for CPU-heavy compilation, high-throughput web serving, or memory-write-intensive database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services g6e.xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a 250 GB NVMe SSD. Its primary accelerator is a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPU with 45 GB of VRAM. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier performance for LLM inference, achieving top 10% speeds in prompt processing and text generation for both 135M and 7B models. While single-core CPU performance is strong, multi-core processing and memory write bandwidth are relatively weak. This hardware configuration is highly efficient for GPU-bound workloads that require substantial VRAM but modest CPU resources, such as machine learning inference and graphics rendering, rather than CPU-intensive compilation or high-throughput database tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, a 250 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU with 45 GB VRAM, optimized for LLM inference and rendering.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.xlarge features 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized instance featuring an NVIDIA L40S GPU with 45 GB VRAM and dedicated AMD EPYC processing for efficient model inference.
bullet_points: NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPU with 45 GB VRAM for hardware acceleration.
4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor at 3.4 GHz.
32 GB of DDR4 system memory and a local 250 GB NVMe SSD.
Top-tier performance in small and medium LLM inference benchmarks.
Best suited for machine learning inference and graphics-intensive workloads.
